title: Forklift Driver
aboutEmployer: Our client is a well-established agricultural processing and logistics operation in Regional NSW, operating year-round with a peak during harvest season. They pride themselves on safe working practices and have a friendly, inclusive team culture.
description: Experienced forklift driver needed for busy harvest season. Must have valid LO/LF licence. You will be responsible for moving, storing, and distributing agricultural produce within a busy warehouse environment.
responsibilities: Safely operate forklift (LO/LF licensed) in a busy warehouse environment
Load and unload produce from trucks and storage areas
Conduct pre-start safety checks on the forklift daily
Maintain accurate stock movement records
Adhere to WHS policies and site-specific safety procedures
qualifications: Valid LO or LF Forklift Licence (mandatory)
Previous experience in agricultural or warehouse environments (preferred)
Ability to work in varying weather conditions
Strong attention to safety and detail
Reliable transport to regional work site

title: Aged Care Worker
aboutEmployer: Our client is a reputable residential aged care facility located in the Sydney Metro area. They are committed to delivering high-quality, person-centred care and support to elderly residents, maintaining a compassionate and professional team environment.
description: Passionate care worker required for residential facility. Certificate III in Individual Support essential. You will support elderly residents with daily living activities, ensure their comfort and dignity, and build meaningful relationships with residents and their families.
responsibilities: Assist residents with personal care including bathing, dressing, and mobility
Administer medications under supervision (where qualified)
Monitor and report on resident health and wellbeing
Support activities of daily living and recreational programs
Maintain accurate care documentation and handover notes
Communicate effectively with residents, families, and healthcare professionals
qualifications: Certificate III in Individual Support (Ageing) – mandatory
Current First Aid and CPR certificate
National Police Check (or willingness to obtain)
NDIS Worker Screening Check (or willingness to obtain)
Strong empathy, patience, and communication skills
Ability to work rotating roster including weekends

title: Civil Labourer
aboutEmployer: Our client is a leading civil infrastructure contractor engaged on a significant government-funded road and drainage project in Western Sydney. They maintain strong safety standards and offer a professional, structured site environment with a diverse and collaborative team.
description: General labourers wanted for a large infrastructure project in Western Sydney. White card and own PPE required. You will support civil construction activities including excavation, concrete works, and general site maintenance under the supervision of a site foreman.
responsibilities: Assist with excavation, concrete pours, and drainage installation
Operate hand tools and basic plant equipment as directed
Maintain site cleanliness and ensure materials are correctly stored
Follow all WHS and site-specific safety policies
Assist in traffic control setup and signage where required
Report hazards and near-misses to the site foreman promptly
qualifications: White Card (Construction Induction) – mandatory
Own appropriate PPE (hi-vis, steel cap boots, hard hat)
Previous civil construction or labouring experience preferred
Ability to work outdoors in varying weather conditions
Physical fitness to perform manual labour tasks
Reliable transport to Western Sydney site locations

title: Chef de Partie
aboutEmployer: Our client is a celebrated restaurant group operating multiple award-winning venues across Melbourne's CBD. Known for their innovative menus and exceptional dining experiences, they invest heavily in team development, offer a supportive kitchen culture, and are committed to the wellbeing and career growth of their chefs.
description: Join an award-winning kitchen team as a Chef de Partie. You will manage a specific station in a high-volume fine-dining kitchen, mentor junior staff, and contribute creatively to seasonal menu development. Minimum 2 years of experience in a similar role is required.
responsibilities: Take ownership and manage a designated kitchen station (e.g., grill, pastry, larder)
Prepare, cook, and present dishes to the highest standard
Supervise and mentor Commis and Demi-Chef team members
Assist the Sous Chef and Head Chef with menu development and specials
Ensure food safety, HACCP compliance, and kitchen hygiene standards
Manage mise en place efficiently during service periods
qualifications: Trade qualification in Commercial Cookery (or equivalent international qualification)
Minimum 2 years' experience as a Chef de Partie in a professional kitchen
Strong knowledge of modern cooking techniques and food trends
Food Safety Supervisor Certificate
Ability to work evenings, weekends, and split shifts
Excellent teamwork and communication skills under pressure
